concerns.text: FR-003 contains a placeholder marker '[deferred]' within the requirement text: 'sampling [deferred] of gradient steps'. This violates the internal consistency rule that requirements must be mutually compatible and complete; a requirement cannot mandate a sampling strategy while simultaneously deferring the definition of that strategy's scope. This contradicts the 'Converged Spec' definition which forbids [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] or [deferred] markers in functional requirements.

The Assumptions section states: 'The SVD computation... is performed using layer-wise randomized SVD ([deferred] sampling)'. This repeats the unresolved placeholder found in FR-003. A converged spec must resolve the sampling strategy (e.g., 'all steps' or 'a fixed window') or explicitly state the metric to be measured, rather than deferring the definition of the input data scope for a core algorithm.
